popularStory.headline: History of Edmund Tapp (ca. 1595 - 1653)
popularStory.description:
by Garry Bryant

Edmund Tapp
Judge
(abt. 1595 - 1653)

From Bennington parish, Hertfordshire, England, came Edmund Tapp (or Tappe) and his family. Tapp in Welsh is a place name meaning “hanging rock.” …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Edmund Tapp Jr.於1578年1月30日出生在英國賀特福爾貝寧頓時，他的父親Edmund Tapp Sr.28歲，母親Margaret19歲。他於1612年與Mary Anne Charde在英國賀特福爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他去世於1653年4月14日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文米爾福德享年75歲，埋葬在美國康乃狄克州劍橋大學默里 哈文米爾福德米爾福德 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Tapp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the early Middle English personal name Tapp (Old English Tæppa). The unrecorded Old English personal name is thought to be the first element of the placenames Taplow (Buckinghamshire), Tapners (Kent), Tappington (Kent), and Tapton (Derbyshire). See also Taplin .
English: possibly a nickname from Middle English tape ‘ribbon, tape’, or tappe ‘plug, tap’; ‘tap, knock’.

variants.name: Tappe
meanings: North German: metonymic occupational name for the owner of a tavern, a beer or wine tapper, from Middle Low German tappe ‘tap’.
English: variant of   Tapp  . This surname has now died out in Britain.

variants.name: Taplin
meanings: probably from Middle English Tolpayn, which may be a nickname for a miller, from Middle English tollen ‘to exact a charge on (something)’ + Middle English (Old French) pain ‘bread’, though the tolls that the miller exacted were on the flour he ground not the bread that was made from it. Alternatively, if -payne is a spelling of Middle English (Old French) peine ‘tax’, it may instead be a nickname for a tax collector.

variants.name: Tapscott
meanings: English (Devon): habitational name from a lost Devon placename, perhaps in or near Barnstaple, recorded as Tappyscote in 1504–15. The placename may derive from an early Middle English personal name Tapp (Old English Tæppa), genitive Tappes, + Middle English cot ‘cottage, hut, shelter’, but this cannot be certain due to a lack of medieval forms. See also Tapp for the Middle English personal name Tapp, which is most frequent in Devon.

nameMeanings.name: Edmund
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + mund ‘protector’. It was borne by several early royal and saintly figures, including a 9th-century king of East Anglia killed by invading Danes, allegedly for his adherence to Christianity. In the 16th and 17th centuries, there was a good deal of interchange between Edmund and Edward.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Edmund Tapp Jr. was born on 30 January 1578, in Benington, Hertfordshire, England, his father, Edmund Tapp Sr., was 28 and his mother, Margaret, was 19. He married Mary Anne Charde in 1612, in Hertfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 7 daughters. He died on 14 April 1653, in Milford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 75, and was buried in Milford Cemetery, Milford, New Haven, Connecticut, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Edmund Tapp Jr. est né le 30 janvier 1578, à Benington, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Edmund Tapp Sr., avait 28 ans et sa mère, Margaret, avait 19 ans. Il a épousé Mary Anne Charde en 1612, à Hertfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 7 filles. Il est décédé le 14 avril 1653, à Milford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 75 ans, et a été enterré à Milford Cemetery, Milford, New Haven, Connecticut, États-Unis.

ko.Milford Cemetery, Milford, New Haven, Co: 미국 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐 미퍼드 미퍼드 세멧리

ko.lifeSummary: Edmund Tapp Jr.(이)가 1578년 1월 30일 영국 허트퍼드실르 베닝턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Edmund Tapp Sr.(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret(은)는 19세였다. 그는 1612년 영국 허트퍼드실르에서 Mary Anne Charde(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1653년 4월 14일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐 미퍼드에서 75세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐 미퍼드 미퍼드 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Edmund Tapp Jr. nasceu em 30 de janeiro de 1578, em Benington, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Edmund Tapp Sr., tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Margaret, tinha 19 anos. Ele casou-se com Mary Anne Charde em 1612, em Hertfordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 7 filhas. Ele faleceu em 14 de abril de 1653, em Milford, New Haven, Conecticute, British Colonial America, com 75 anos, e foi sepultado em Milford Cemetery, Milford, New Haven, Conecticute, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edmund Tapp Jr. родился 30 января 1578 г. в местности бенингтон, хиртфордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Edmund Tapp Sr. было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret, 19. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Anne Charde в 1612 г. в местности хиртфордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 7 дочерей. Он умер 14 апреля 1653 г. в местности милфорд, ню хавен, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 75 лет и был похоронен в местности милфорд кеметири, милфорд, ню хавен, Коннектикут, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Edmund Tapp Jr. nació el 30 de enero de 1578, en Benington, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Edmund Tapp Sr., tenía 28 años y su mamá, Margaret, tenía 19 años. Se casó con Mary Anne Charde en 1612, en Hertfordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió el 14 de abril de 1653, en Milford, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 75 años, y fue sepultado en Milford Cemetery, Milford, New Haven, Connecticut, Estados Unidos.
